steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The expression $(x+8)^2 + 14(x+8) + 49$ is a trinomial with a compound base. Recognize whether it fits the perfect-square pattern $u^2 + 14u + 49 = (u+7)^2$ by setting $u = x+8$.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — $ (x + 15)^2 $: substituting $u = x+8$ gives $(u+7)^2 = (x+8+7)^2 = (x+15)^2$. ✓
  • Choice B — $ (x + 7)^2 $: this would come from using $x$ as the base instead of $x+8$. ✗
  • Choice C — $ 8(x + 7)^2 $: this adds an outside factor of 8 and uses the wrong binomial. ✗
  • Choice D — $ 7(x + 8)^2 $: this keeps the base $x+8$ but treats the expression like a multiple, not a completed square. ✗
A is correct.
